description: The Messina Brothers were five Maltese brothers who led a criminal organization in London from the 1930s to the 1950s, primarily involved in prostitution. They were born to Giuseppe Messina, who established brothels in Malta and Egypt. The brothers expanded their operations to London's Soho district, controlling a large network of prostitutes and bribing police. Their criminal activities declined after the 1950s.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: London in England, Malta , Alexandria in Egypt Sanremo in Italy and Brussels in Belgium
country_links.context: The organization had territory in Brussels, Belgium.

country_links.evidence_quote: He then moved to Alexandria in Egypt in 1905 where there was a thriving Maltese community and built a chain of brothels. The other three sons Eugenio (born 1908, Alexandria, Egypt), Attillo (born 1910, Alexandria) and Carmelo (born 1915, Alexandria) were born in Egypt. All the five brothers joined their father in running brothels and prostitution.
country_links.context: The Messina brothers' father built a chain of brothels in Egypt, and three brothers were born there; the brothers joined their father in running brothels and prostitution in Egypt.

country_links.evidence_quote: imported women from Belgium, France and Spain.
country_links.context: The Messina brothers imported women from France for their prostitution operation.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1934 Eugenio the third son of Giuseppe, moved to London with his French prostitute wife Colette. She helped establish the sex trade in London. The other Messina brothers followed Eugenio and established themselves in Soho, London.
country_links.context: The Messina brothers established themselves in Soho, London, ran prostitution operations, and imported women from other countries into the UK.
